name: First Government Junta
short_description: Provisional self-government breaks from Spanish viceregal authority.

name: Independence proclaimed
short_description: Battle of Maipú secures independence from Spain.

name: Manuel Blanco Encalada inaugurated
short_description: Liberal naval officer; the first to hold the title President of Chile, in a brief two-month term.

name: Francisco Antonio Pinto inaugurated
short_description: Liberal (pipiolo); presided over the short-lived federalist experiment and the 1828 Constitution.

name: José Joaquín Prieto inaugurated
short_description: Conservative (pelucón); opened the thirty-year Conservative Republic and the 1833 Constitution.

name: Constitution of 1833
short_description: Conservative republic; centralized presidency, restricted suffrage.

name: Manuel Bulnes inaugurated
short_description: Conservative; victorious general of the war against the Peru-Bolivian Confederation, then a decade of stability and southern settlement.

name: Manuel Montt inaugurated
short_description: Conservative civilian; railways, schools, and the Civil Code, though his rule provoked the 1851 and 1859 revolts.

name: Civil War
short_description: Liberal revolution of 1859; Copiapó and the north rise against the Santiago government.

name: Liberal era begins
short_description: Pérez assumes presidency; Liberal-Conservative coalition.

name: José Joaquín Pérez inaugurated
short_description: Moderate of the Liberal-Conservative fusion; his calm decade opened the Liberal era.

name: Federico Errázuriz Zañartu inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; governed through the Liberal-Radical alliance, the first president barred from re-election.

name: Suffrage reform
short_description: Property requirement loosened; literate-male suffrage.

name: Aníbal Pinto inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; faced the 1870s export slump and led Chile into the War of the Pacific.

name: War of the Pacific
short_description: Chile annexes Tarapacá and Antofagasta; nitrate boom begins.

name: Domingo Santa María inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; passed the anticlerical leyes laicas secularizing the civil registry, marriage, and cemeteries.

name: Treaty of Ancón
short_description: Ends War of the Pacific; cements territorial gains.

name: José Manuel Balmaceda inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; nitrate revenue funded vast public works, but his clash with Congress brought the 1891 civil war.

name: Universal male suffrage
short_description: Property qualification removed; voter registers expand.

name: Civil War of 1891
short_description: Congressionalists defeat Balmaceda; parliamentary republic.

name: Jorge Montt inaugurated
short_description: Navy admiral who led the Congressionalist victory; opened the Parliamentary Republic.

name: Federico Errázuriz Echaurren inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; governed the early Parliamentary Republic and moved to settle the Argentine border dispute.

name: Germán Riesco inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; signed the 1902 Pactos de Mayo settling the boundary with Argentina.

name: Pedro Montt inaugurated
short_description: National Party; presided over reconstruction after the 1906 Valparaíso earthquake.

name: Ramón Barros Luco inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; centennial-era figure of the Parliamentary Republic's rotating cabinets.

name: Juan Luis Sanfuentes inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; last president of the Parliamentary Republic, through the WWI nitrate boom and bust.

name: Alessandri elected
short_description: Reform mandate ends parliamentary republic.

name: Arturo Alessandri inaugurated
short_description: Liberal Alliance reformer, the 'León de Tarapacá', elected on a social-reform mandate against the parliamentary oligarchy.

name: Military coup
short_description: Officer revolt forces Alessandri into exile.

name: Constitution of 1925
short_description: Presidential system restored; church-state separation.

name: Emiliano Figueroa inaugurated
short_description: Liberal caretaker president, soon sidelined by the army and forced to resign in 1927.

name: Carlos Ibáñez del Campo inaugurated
short_description: Army colonel; ruled by authoritarian fiat with ambitious public works until the 1931 crisis toppled him.

name: Great Depression
short_description: Nitrate market collapses; export economy contracts ~50%.

name: Juan Esteban Montero inaugurated
short_description: Radical; elected as the Depression hit bottom and overthrown within a year.

name: Socialist Republic
short_description: Brief military-backed socialist regime.

name: Arturo Alessandri inaugurated
short_description: Liberal; returned to restore constitutional order after the turmoil of 1932 and steer an orthodox recovery.

name: Popular Front elected
short_description: Aguirre Cerda leads Radical-led center-left coalition.

name: Pedro Aguirre Cerda inaugurated
short_description: Radical; led the Popular Front of Radicals, Socialists, and Communists, and founded CORFO to drive state industrialization.

name: Juan Antonio Ríos inaugurated
short_description: Radical; wartime president of the developmentalist drive, 'to govern is to produce'.

name: Gabriel González Videla inaugurated
short_description: Radical; elected with Communist support, then outlawed the party under the 1948 'Ley Maldita'.

name: Female suffrage (national)
short_description: Women obtain national voting rights.

name: Carlos Ibáñez del Campo inaugurated
short_description: Returned as an elected populist above party, campaigning on a broom to sweep out corruption.

name: Single official ballot
short_description: Adoption of the single state-printed ballot, ending party-printed votes and curbing electoral bribery.

name: Jorge Alessandri inaugurated
short_description: Independent backed by the right; a businessman who ran an orthodox, technocratic administration.

name: Agrarian Reform Law
short_description: Alessandri's first agrarian reform statute (Law 15.020).

name: Frei elected
short_description: Christian Democratic 'Revolution in Liberty' begins.

name: Eduardo Frei Montalva inaugurated
short_description: Christian Democrat; the 'Revolution in Liberty' of agrarian reform and copper 'Chileanization'.

name: Frei agrarian reform
short_description: Law 16.640 expropriates large estates.

name: Allende elected
short_description: Popular Unity wins narrowly; nationalization accelerates.

name: Salvador Allende inaugurated
short_description: Socialist of the Popular Unity coalition; the first Marxist freely elected president in the Americas.

name: Copper nationalization
short_description: Constitutional amendment transfers Gran Minería.

name: Military coup
short_description: Pinochet junta overthrows Allende; congress dissolved.

name: Chicago Boys reforms
short_description: Trade liberalization, deregulation, monetarist shock.

name: Constitution of 1980
short_description: Pinochet plebiscite ratifies authoritarian charter.

name: Debt crisis
short_description: Banking collapse; GDP contracts ~14%.

name: Plebiscite NO wins
short_description: Pinochet rejected; transition to democracy scheduled.

name: Democratic transition
short_description: Aylwin assumes presidency; civilian rule restored.

name: Patricio Aylwin inaugurated
short_description: Christian Democrat of the Concertación; first president of the post-Pinochet democratic transition.
